response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong web, full-stack, and some systems-level background, but the core requirement of this role is deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His only professional C++ work was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; his career since has centered on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial protocol experience, making him a poor fit despite his overall seniority.
strengths: Strong low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby, albeit in DLang rather than C++
Solid engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with event-driven platform design
concerns: Minimal and outdated professional C++ experience (brief role ending in 2015); C++ not even listed in his skills section
No industrial cybersecurity exposure: no IEC 62443, SDL, or secure-development-lifecycle background
No MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or industrial automation/IoT protocol experience; recent work is web/product-focused

key_factors.factor: Strong general systems and event-driven engineering experience
